feed.title: Bitcoin Core v29.4rc1 is now available

entry.title: Bitcoin Core v29.4rc1 is now available
entry.summary:
The latest release candidate for Bitcoin Core, labeled version v29.4rc1, is now available for download. This version marks a minor version release and can be accessed through binaries hosted at [Bitcoin Core’s official site](https://bitcoincore.org/bin/bitcoin-core-29.4/test.rc1/). Additionally, the source code for this release is accessible via a signed tag on GitHub, providing transparency and security for users wishing to view or contribute to the development process. The specific tag can be found at [GitHub - bitcoin/bitcoin/tree/v29.4rc1](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/tree/v29.4rc1).
This release candidate serves as a test version before the final release. It aims to identify and rectify any critical issues prior to the official launch of version 29.4. To aid in understanding the changes and enhancements included in this release, preliminary release notes have been made available at [GitHub Release Notes](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/blob/v29.4rc1/doc/release-notes.md). Users encountering any problems with this release candidate are encouraged to report them directly to the project’s issue tracker on GitHub at [Report Issues](https://github.com/bitcoin/bitcoin/issues).
The distribution of this notification was facilitated through the Google Groups "Bitcoin Development Mailing List," which targets subscribers involved or interested in the development updates of Bitcoin Core.
